title: Crispy Fujian Cabbage Stir‑fry
description: A salty, crisp weeknight Fujian dish featuring shredded savoy cabbage and tender chicken tossed in a savory soy‑oyster sauce.
instructions: 1. Rinse and shred the savoy cabbage, then pat dry with a clean towel.
2. Cut the chicken into bite‑size cubes and set aside.
3. In a wok or large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat until shimmering.
4. Add the minced garlic, ginger and sliced chili; stir‑fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
5. Increase the heat to high and toss in the chicken cubes; cook for 4–5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the chicken is lightly browned and cooked through.
6. Add the shredded cabbage, a splash of water or chicken stock, and the rice wine; stir‑fry for 3–4 minutes, letting the cabbage wilt yet remain crisp.
7. Reduce the heat to medium, pour in the soy sauce, oyster sauce, and a pinch of white pepper; stir well to coat all ingredients.
8. Continue cooking 2 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly; sprinkle toasted sesame seeds and give a final stir.
9. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary, then transfer the stir‑fry to a serving bowl.
